name: CHD - Diagnosis + Procedure via _has (Interventional Subset)
description: **Cross-resource _has test combining Condition + Procedure.** Find CHD patients who have BOTH a CHD diagnosis AND have undergone a revascularization procedure (CABG or PCI). This is the interventional-management subset, distinct from medical-management-only patients. Tests whether LLM uses FHIR `_has` to combine diagnosis evidence with procedural evidence — a different cross-resource pairing than the more common Condition + MedicationRequest.
prompt: Find coronary heart disease patients who have BOTH a formal CHD diagnosis AND have undergone a revascularization procedure (CABG, PCI, coronary stent placement, or balloon angioplasty). The intent is the interventional-management cohort — patients whose CHD has been treated procedurally, not just medically.

prompts.broad: Find patients who have BOTH a coronary heart disease diagnosis AND have undergone a coronary revascularization procedure. Use FHIR `_has` to combine evidence from Condition and Procedure resources at the patient level.
prompts.expert: I need patients matching TWO criteria: a Condition with a CHD SNOMED code (53741008, 414545008, 233817007, 371803003, 371804009) AND a Procedure with a revascularization SNOMED code (232717009 CABG, 415070008 PCI, 36969009 stent, 68466008 balloon). Use `Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=...&_has:Procedure:patient:code=...`. Note Procedure resources are typically scarcer than Condition or MedicationRequest in real EHRs — the diagnosis filter is necessary because some procedure codes also appear in screening contexts.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Cross-resource intersection via FHIR `_has` combining Condition + Procedure. First test pairing these two specific resource types via `_has`.

required_codes.display: Triple vessel disease of the heart
Placement of stent in coronary artery
